response_text:
To answer the math problem included in your text:

1.  **First 3 hours:** Rosie runs 10 mph.
    $10 \text{ mph} \times 3 \text{ hours} = 30 \text{ miles}$.
2.  **Remaining 4 hours:** (7 total hours - 3 initial hours) Rosie runs 5 mph.
    $5 \text{ mph} \times 4 \text{ hours} = 20 \text{ miles}$.
3.  **Total distance:**
    $30 \text{ miles} + 20 \text{ miles} = \mathbf{50 \text{ miles}}$.

Rosie can run **50 miles** in 7 hours.
